name: One-way ANOVA with post-hoc tests and effect sizes
prompt: Read experiment_data.csv with columns: group (A/B/C) and score (numeric). Perform a complete statistical analysis: (1) compute descriptive statistics (mean, std, n) per group, (2) run a one-way ANOVA to test if groups differ, (3) if significant, run post-hoc pairwise t-tests with Bonferroni correction, (4) compute Cohen's d effect size for each pairwise comparison, (5) determine required sample size for 80% power to detect the observed A-B difference. Write stats_results.json with: descriptives (array of {group, mean, std, n}), anova ({f_statistic, p_value, significant}), pairwise (array of {group1, group2, t_statistic, p_value, p_adjusted, significant, cohens_d}), power_analysis ({effect_size, required_n_per_group}).

criteria.description: stats_results.json exists and is valid JSON
Descriptive statistics per group are correct
ANOVA is run and reported as significant
Pairwise A-B is significant after Bonferroni correction
Pairwise A-C is NOT significant after Bonferroni correction
Cohen's d for A-B is in medium-to-large range
Power analysis reports a positive required_n_per_group

origin.notes: auto-converted from legacy skill-bench TaskDefinition by tools/convert-from-skill-bench.ts
